title: Rootless container running squid show slirp4netns IP instead of client IP in log files.
body:
### Issue Description

I have deployed a squid proxy in a rootless container. I have configured the log format of squid to contain "Client source IP address". 
However when monitoring the logs all the source IPs are '10.0.2.100', which I believe is the default IP address for rootless containers using slirp4netns
Is there a way I can pass the client source IPs into the slirp4netns so squid can record the correct IP address in it's logs.
Running on RHEL 8.7 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Creating container using the following command
`podman run -dt --name squid_proxy -p 8124:3128 -v /home/apps/containers/squid_patch/conf/squid.conf:/etc/squid/squid.conf:Z -v /home/apps/containers/squid_patch/conf/conf.d:/etc/squid/conf.d:Z docker.io/ubuntu/squid:latest
`
Followed the logs using `podman logs -f squid_proxy` 
Observed the results in the logs

### Describe the results you received

Snippet of log file that shows the 10.0.2.100 IP address
10.0.2.100 60572 [14/Jun/2023:00:10:22 +0000] "CONNECT sccm.manage.microsoft.co
10.0.2.100 60558 [14/Jun/2023:00:12:02 +0000] "CONNECT go.microsoft.com:443 HTT
10.0.2.100 33608 [14/Jun/2023:00:13:54 +0000] "GET http://ocsp.digicert.com/MFE
10.0.2.100 33612 [14/Jun/2023:00:13:54 +0000] "GET http://crl4.digicert.com/Dig
10.0.2.100 33612 [14/Jun/2023:00:13:54 +0000] "GET http://crl3.digicert.com/Dig


### Describe the results you expected

Log file showing the real client IP address of hosts contecting to the container.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

comments.body:
I figured out this issue, I needed to run the container with the following switch

`--network slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns` 

I found the solution in this KB article from Redhat.
**Why rootless containers are not able to see the source ip address in Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8**
https://access.redhat.com/solutions/6962104
